notes: Excellent alignment between the build-out topic and the positional structure (back four + pivot).
Constraints are purposeful and age-appropriate for U14 competitive players.
The defensive pressing cues are clear but leave room for guided discovery.
Session duration and work:rest structure are realistic for a main block within a 75–90 minute training.
qa.summary: A clean, game-realistic 6v4 + GK build-out exercise in the middle third that emphasizes positional play, pressing cues, and playing through pressure. Well-structured for U14 with clear constraints and coaching focus.

json.setup: Use half a pitch, oriented horizontally. Mark the middle third from the top of the penalty area to slightly past midfield. One full-size goal for the build-out team, two mini-goals at the halfway line for the pressing team. Balls with the coach beside the goal.
json.title: Midfield Build-Out 6v4 + GK

teamShape.notes: Attacking team uses a back four plus single pivot and one advanced CM. Pressing team sets a front two with wide screeners to lock play to one side.

roleUsage.notes: Diagram shows the back four, pivot and one CM for the build-out, plus a 2+2 pressing structure for the defending team.

coach.label: Coach
coach.note: Coach starts play with GK or CB from the goal area.

loadNotes.rationale: Focused main block for build-out principles with repeated exposure under moderate pressure.
loadNotes.structure: 4 x 4-minute sets, 2-minute coaching/rotation (24 minutes total including rest).

json.sessionObjective: Coach the back four and pivot to build through the middle third against a 2+2 press while maintaining composure and finding the free player.
constraints: Attacking team must involve the pivot (DM) at least once before playing beyond the midline.
Goals for the pressing team (DEF) only count from counter-attacks into the mini-goals within 6 seconds of winning the ball.
If the attacking team loses the ball, they must immediately counter-press for 3 seconds before dropping.
GK must play short (no clipped balls beyond the halfway line on first action).
json.description: A 6v4 + GK build-out scenario in the middle third where the back four and pivot learn to open passing lanes, recognize pressure cues, and play through or around a compact front four press. The defending team practices coordinated triggers, steering play to one side and attacking quickly into mini-goals.
progression: Allow one extra midfielder from the attacking team to join from the far side after the first pass to create a 7v4 and reward switches of play.
Tighten time constraints for the build-out (must cross the midline within 10 seconds) to train quicker circulation under pressure.
json.organization: Format: 6v4 + GK. Area: Half pitch, focused on the middle third. Attacking team (blue) builds from the GK and back four with a pivot and one CM. Defending team (red) presses with a front two and two wide screeners. Full-size goal for the build-out team, two mini-goals at the halfway line for the pressing team.
progressions: Add a second pivot (another CM) to create a 7v4 overload and challenge the defending team to press higher or drop into a mid-block.
Flip the pressing direction after several reps so the build-out team must learn to solve the problem on both sides.
scoringHints: 1 point for progressing beyond the midline in control.
2 points for a goal scored after breaking the first pressing line with a vertical pass.
2 points for the defending team scoring into a mini-goal within 6 seconds of winning the ball.
coachingPoints: GK and CBs: Body shape to see both sides, first touch away from pressure, play the way you face if pressed.
DM: Constantly adjust angle and distance to be the free player; scan before receiving; play one or two-touch to accelerate circulation.
Fullbacks: Start high and wide, time drops to receive on the half-turn; recognize when to step in with the ball.
Pressing team: Curved runs to screen the pivot, clear communication of triggers (poor touch, backwards pass, or bounce into the GK).

qa.summary: Demo QA wrapper for the ACI frontend. This drill is suitable as a static example for the diagram and card layout.
notes: Static demo drill JSON, not yet sourced from the live API.
Includes teamShape, numbersOnField, and roleUsage for future backend wiring.

fixDecision.reason: Static vetted example for UI demo; already in high-quality state.

decision.reason: No auto-fix applied for static demo drill.
